g.text: NAIVE DUAL WRITE u2014 the problem we're trying to solve

text.tspan: FAILURE MODE A:
DB commit SUCCEEDS, Kafka publish FAILS
u2192 order EXISTS in DB
u2192 downstream services never hear about it
u2192 INCONSISTENT: shipping, billing, analytics all miss this order
FAILURE MODE B:
DB commit SUCCEEDS, Kafka publish SUCCEEDS,
but process CRASHES before client got the ACK
u2192 client retries u2192 ORDER DUPLICATED (DB + Kafka)
u2192 BUT if we add idempotency:
client retries with same key u2192 DB rejects duplicate u2192 but ALSO skips publish
u2192 second message LOST
FAILURE MODE C:
Kafka publish goes first, then DB commit FAILS
u2192 message PUBLISHED but order NEVER persisted
u2192 downstream services act on a non-existent order
ROOT CAUSE: there is no DISTRIBUTED TRANSACTION across the DB and Kafka.
We're writing to two separate systems; ATOMICITY is impossible without 2PC
(which Kafka doesn't support, and which has its own problems).
SOLUTION: write to ONE system atomically u2014 the database.
Then RELIABLY DERIVE the message from the database state.
u2192 This is the OUTBOX pattern.
BEGIN;
INSERT INTO orders ...;
COMMIT;
kafka.publish('OrderPlaced', ...); // separate I/O
